bird.name: 禿鸛
bird.summary: 秃鹳（台湾作小禿鸛）（学名：Leptoptilos javanicus），是鹳形目鹳科的一种鸟类。 特征 秃鹳体重约4650.99克，翼长约624.6毫米，嘴峰长约280.5毫米，喙宽度约37.5毫米，喙厚度约59.7毫米，跗蹠长约249.6毫米，尾长约277.2毫米。秃鹳是部…

relations.subject: 禿鸛
relations.evidence: thumb|In Sri Lanka The lesser adjutant is often found in large rivers and lakes inside well wooded regions, in freshwater wetlands in agricultural areas, and coastal wetlands including mudflats and mangroves.{{Cite journal|last1=Subaraj|first1=R.|last2=Lok|first2=A
relations.object_summary: 海湾、泻湖、盐沼与河口组成的复合湿地。

relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 禿鸛
relations.evidence: Soc.|year=2003| volume=100| issue=1| page=111|url=https://biodiversitylibrary.org/page/48602685 }} In Sri Lanka, they are found in lowland areas largely within protected areas, though they also use forested wetlands and crop fields. In Nepal, surveys in eastern districts had suggested that they preferentially use forested patches with small wetlands, largely avoiding crop fields. Additional studies, however, are showing the opposite - breeding densities and breeding success of Lesser Adjutant across lowland Nepal are much higher on croplands.{{Cite journal|last1=Sundar|first1=K.S.G
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 禿鸛
relations.evidence: religiosa respectively. Other tree species on which nests have been found in India and Myanmar include Alstonia scholaris and Salmalia malabarica with some nests located as high as 46 m. Nests have not yet been located in Sri Lanka, though young birds have been observed feeding in crop fields and in freshwater wetlands. The average size of 35 colonies with a total of 101 nests in central, lowland Nepal was 2.9 nests, ranging in size from one nest to 13 nests. Location of colonies in central lowland Nepal was not related to tree density available on the landscape suggesting that nest trees are still adequate here
relations.object_summary: 浅水、沼泽与植被斑块组成的高生产力生态系统。

relations.subject: 禿鸛
relations.evidence: Lesser adjutants were downlisted from Vulnerable to Near Threatened by the IUCN in 2023, due to community efforts to protect their nests and an expected slowing of population declines. In Nepal, lesser adjutants appear to be more adaptable to human habituation than their congenerics (e.g., Asian openbills), given that some wetland habitats are retained. While lesser adjutants appear to breed successfully in agricultural land, they are threatened by increasing urbanization, and the loss of tree species they prefer to nest in, such as Bombax ceiba, Haldina cordifolia, and Ficus religiosa. Like many other large waterbirds, the southeast Asian populations of lesser adjutant storks appear to be greatly at risk due to a combination of hunting and habitat destruction.{{Cite book |last=Goes |first=F
relations.object_summary: 开发、采伐和围垦等活动导致适宜生境减少。
